item.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
item.description: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

item.title: 26 Meta workers sue over alleged AI-aided layoffs of employees on leave
item.description: A group of 26 Meta employees has sued the company, claiming it used AI to choose people for layoffs, disproportionately targeting those on medical, parental or family leave.

item.title: Houston prosecutor could bring charges against ICE agents in fatal shooting
item.description: Harris County District Attorney Sean Teare, who is investigating the fatal ICE shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, told CBS News ICE's tactics "in no way resemble" the behavior of police agencies he's worked with.

item.title: Blanche to testify today at Senate confirmation hearing for attorney general
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is set to appear before the Senate Judiciary Committee at 9 a.m. for his confirmation hearing to lead the Justice Department in a permanent capacity.

item.title: Jay Clayton, Trump's DNI nominee, to testify at Senate confirmation hearing
item.description: Clayton, currently the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, is expected to succeed Bill Pulte, Trump's controversial acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Iraq seizes mounds of gold and millions in cash in corruption crackdown
item.description: An anti-corruption probe in Iraq leads to seizure of 825 pounds of gold and tens of millions in cash from lawmakers and government officials.

item.title: 1 dead, 3 missing after boat sinks near Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay
item.description: One person died and three were missing in San Francisco Bay on Tuesday after a boat with 20 people on board sank near Alcatraz Island, authorities said.

item.title: U.S. restarts blockade and strikes Iran as Trump seeks control of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As the U.S. restarts its Iran blockade and carries out more strikes, President Trump has called off a planned 20% fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Flights grounded at D.C.-area airport due to security concerns for Iraqi leader
item.description: The Iraqi leader was in Washington to meet with President Trump.

item.title: Every ICE arrest team will now have an agent with a body camera, DHS says
item.description: Every ICE arrest team will have at least one law enforcement officer equipped with a body-worn camera going forward, DHS said Tuesday, after two fatal shootings by agents who didn't have cameras.

item.title: These are the biggest misconceptions about inherited debt, according to experts
item.description: Many people assume they'll inherit a loved one's debt, but the rules are far more nuanced than you may realize.

item.title: After ICE fatally shot multiple people, officials speak out: "We want them out"
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials shot and killed two men in the last week in Maine and in Texas. Maine's House speaker, a Houston prosecutor and a Minnesota attorney spoke with CBS News about the violence.

item.title: Anthropic launches "Claude for Teachers," giving educators a year of free access to AI tools
item.description: Anthropic is offering verified teachers a year of free access to its premium AI tools through its new "Claude for Teachers" program. Elizabeth Kelly, head of Beneficial Deployments at Anthopic, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Houston prosecutor says he's trying to get visas for witnesses of fatal ICE shooting
item.description: Harris County District Attorney Sean Teare, who is investigating the ICE killing of Mexican immigrant Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, says his office is filing paperwork to get visas for the witnesses of the fatal shooting, so they're not deported while the investigation unfolds.

item.title: Utility companies requested $9.2 billion in rate increases during second quarter, report shows
item.description: Utility companies requested $9.2 billion worth of rate increases in the second quarter of 2026, potentially impacting over 56 million Americans, according to a new report from PowerLines. Former Michigan utility commissioner Tremaine Phillips joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Security video captures moments after man was fatally shot by ICE in Maine
item.description: A home security camera captured the sound of five gunshots when Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, a Colombian national, was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: Spain reaches World Cup final with dominant 2-0 win over France
item.description: In a match Tuesday featuring two of soccer's biggest heavyweights, Spain put in a masterful performance, frustrating France to the tune of a 2-0 win to advance to Sunday's World Cup final​.

item.title: 7/14: CBS Evening News
item.description: More than 20 inches of rain are forecasted for parts of Texas; Immigration and Customs Enforcement changes its tactics after deadly shootings.

item.title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: The measure also allows states to opt out if they take action before the federal law is enacted.

item.title: 7/14: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Latest on ICE scrutiny after fatal shootings in Texas and Maine; aide to Rep. Ro Khanna on moment he says he was detained in the West Bank.

item.title: Scammers are using FaceTime to steal bank account passwords
item.description: The scheme begins with fake fraud alerts before shifting to a FaceTime call, where victims are tricked into exposing sensitive banking information.

item.title: E. Jean Carroll receives $5M from Trump in abuse, defamation case
item.description: A jury concluded in 2023 that Trump should pay Carroll $5 million in damages.

item.title: Trump orders new strikes "to hold Iranian forces accountable," CENTCOM says
item.description: U.S. forces conducted more rounds of strikes on Iran this week, one of which was in retaliation for an attack on a commercial ship in the Strait of Hormuz, the Pentagon said.

item.title: My Mother's Murder Trials - Part 2
item.description: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports for "48 Hours."

item.title: My Mother's Murder Trials - Part 1
item.description: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports for "48 Hours."

item.title: Alleged Capital One hacker threatened to "shoot up" tech company
item.description: The ex-Amazon software engineer took little effort to cover her tracks, even making threats against a company in May

item.title: What we know about accused Capital One hacker Paige Thompson
item.description: Ex-Amazon worker implicated in cyberattack had eight different employers and alluded to emotional problems

item.title: Capital One data breach hits more than 100 million people
item.description: The hacker got information including credit scores and balances plus the Social Security numbers of about 140,000 customers

item.title: Have a Capital One credit card? Take these 5 steps
item.description: If you're a Capital One customer, consider adding extra levels of security to keep scammers from misusing your data
